guest: Jordan Harbinger, podcaster (short intro cross-promo segment only)

business_ideas.idea: Unbundled MBA-as-a-service: paid founder network/cohort like On Deck, selling the career-pivot value of business school
Fixing education — the topic Shaan pitched to Naval on Clubhouse
Fast Grants model: 48-hour funding decisions applied to slow grant-style processes

predictions.text: Clubhouse will be gone in three months; cool gadget, horrible business, not investable
Alt milks will keep growing like crazy and Oatly is the best of them
Countries hardened by adversity will thrive in hard times in ways complacent America will not

disagreements: Sam thinks America can regain urgency after COVID; Shaan argues big institutions can't change culture and calls that a dream. They also split on Clubhouse — Sam dismisses it as a doomed toy while Shaan extracted real value from it. Both agree founder-dating meetups are a bad idea.
dynamics: Shaan arrives with the topic stack ('six bullets in the chamber') and does most of the explaining; Sam reacts with hot takes and Gilded Age history analogies. Fast topic-hopping, high energy, comfortable pushback in both directions.
frameworks: Hard times create strong men cycle: comfort breeds complacency, adversity breeds builders
Underdog positioning (Avis 'We Try Harder'): act like number two, elevate risk profile
Unbundling business school: sell the network and career pause without the degree
Monarchy model: great companies have one boss, not a democracy
learnings: On Deck's real product is a socially acceptable career pause plus a network, not education
Packaging and distribution can outweigh the product itself — RX Bar and Oatly both inflected on a rebrand
A founder running something part-time with an installed operator is a red flag; follow-through beats idea volume
Being attacked by a powerful incumbent (Oatly vs Swedish dairy) can be converted into marketing

banger_quotes.quote: You have to act like you're number 2
I think a lot of great companies are monarchies
Distribution is more important than product

numbers_dropped: On Deck: ~$1M revenue claimed, ~1,000 members paying ~$1,000 per cohort
Oatly: over $200M in sales, cannot meet demand (black market among baristas)
Product Hunt exited to AngelList for ~$20M
Fast Grants: $15M+ committed, $10K-$500K grants decided in under 48 hours
Ethereum ICO ~$17, trading ~$180 at recording, peaked ~$1,000
notable: Sam declares his next company will have no 50-50 co-founder — he wants to own it all and be the monarch.
